steps.title: Read two points from the graph
Backsolving — testing choice B (\$2.60)
steps.content: Assume the cost per additional mile is $2.60. Using the base fare of $5 from the y‑intercept, the predicted cost after 5 miles is $$5 + 2.60 \times 5 = 5 + 13 = 18$$ dollars. The actual cost from the graph at 5 miles is \$15, so this assumption fails.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice A (\$2.00)
steps.content: Assume the cost per additional mile is $2.00. Predicted cost after 5 miles: $$5 + 2.00 \times 5 = 5 + 10 = 15$$ dollars, which exactly matches the graph’s value of \$15 at 5 miles. That matches choice A — A is correct.
